---
title: "Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words"
---

# Databases, Persistence, and Time-Binding Words

Range: `0887-0968`.

Use the summary table for scanning. Each row links to the detailed anchored entry below.

| Sigil | Term                             | Summary                                                                                                                                                            |
| ----- | -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 0887  | [ACID](#rune-0887)               | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0888  | [append-only log](#rune-0888)    | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0889  | [archive table](#rune-0889)      | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0890  | [audit trail](#rune-0890)        | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0891  | [B-tree](#rune-0891)             | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0892  | [backup](#rune-0892)             | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0893  | [base table](#rune-0893)         | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0894  | [bitmap index](#rune-0894)       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0895  | [checkpoint](#rune-0895)         | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0896  | [column {DB}](#rune-0896)        | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0897  | [column store](#rune-0897)       | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0898  | [commit {DB}](#rune-0898)        | The moment a proposed change becomes durable history. Shadow: writing too early, before the world is actually ready to carry the new truth.                        |
| 0899  | [compaction {DB}](#rune-0899)    | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0900  | [conflict](#rune-0900)           | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0901  | [constraint {DB}](#rune-0901)    | A bounding word; it removes degrees of freedom in order to improve correctness.                                                                                    |
| 0902  | [covering index](#rune-0902)     | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0903  | [cursor {DB}](#rune-0903)        | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0904  | [data lake](#rune-0904)          | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0905  | [data warehouse](#rune-0905)     | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0906  | [database](#rune-0906)           | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0907  | [deadlock {DB}](#rune-0907)      | A binding curse; multiple actors wait on one another forever.                                                                                                      |
| 0908  | [denormalization](#rune-0908)    | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0909  | [dimension table](#rune-0909)    | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0910  | [durability](#rune-0910)         | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0911  | [durable log](#rune-0911)        | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0912  | [entity table](#rune-0912)       | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0913  | [event store](#rune-0913)        | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0914  | [fact table](#rune-0914)         | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0915  | [failover replica](#rune-0915)   | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0916  | [foreign key](#rune-0916)        | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0917  | [full table scan](#rune-0917)    | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0918  | [index {DB}](#rune-0918)         | A retrieval word; it makes later access faster by storing an alternate path to data.                                                                               |
| 0919  | [index hint](#rune-0919)         | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0920  | [index seek](#rune-0920)         | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0921  | [isolation level](#rune-0921)    | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0922  | [join {DB}](#rune-0922)          | A union word; separate streams, sets, or histories are brought into one result.                                                                                    |
| 0923  | [keyspace](#rune-0923)           | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0924  | [LSM tree](#rune-0924)           | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0925  | [materialized view](#rune-0925)  | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0926  | [migration](#rune-0926)          | Stored reality changing shape without being lost. Good migrations are staged, inspectable, and reversible long enough to discover what the data actually contains. |
| 0927  | [MVCC](#rune-0927)               | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0928  | [nonclustered index](#rune-0928) | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0929  | [OLAP](#rune-0929)               | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0930  | [OLTP](#rune-0930)               | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0931  | [partition {DB}](#rune-0931)     | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0932  | [partition key](#rune-0932)      | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0933  | [primary key](#rune-0933)        | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0934  | [query plan](#rune-0934)         | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0935  | [read committed](#rune-0935)     | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0936  | [read replica](#rune-0936)       | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0937  | [record lock](#rune-0937)        | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0938  | [redo log](#rune-0938)           | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0939  | [relation {DB}](#rune-0939)      | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0940  | [repeatable read](#rune-0940)    | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0941  | [replication lag](#rune-0941)    | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0942  | [retention](#rune-0942)          | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0943  | [rollback {DB}](#rune-0943)      | Permission to retreat toward a previously trusted state. A system without rollback mistakes hope for safety.                                                       |
| 0944  | [row {DB}](#rune-0944)           | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0945  | [row store](#rune-0945)          | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0946  | [savepoint](#rune-0946)          | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0947  | [scan {DB}](#rune-0947)          | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0948  | [schema {DB}](#rune-0948)        | A shape word for stored meaning.                                                                                                                                   |
| 0949  | [secondary index](#rune-0949)    | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0950  | [seed data](#rune-0950)          | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0951  | [serializable {DB}](#rune-0951)  | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0952  | [sharding](#rune-0952)           | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0953  | [snapshot isolation](#rune-0953) | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0954  | [sort key](#rune-0954)           | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0955  | [stored procedure](#rune-0955)   | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0956  | [table {DB}](#rune-0956)         | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0957  | [table scan](#rune-0957)         | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0958  | [tenant isolation](#rune-0958)   | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0959  | [transaction](#rune-0959)        | An all-or-nothing persistence word.                                                                                                                                |
| 0960  | [TTL](#rune-0960)                | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0961  | [tuple {DB}](#rune-0961)         | A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.                                                                                  |
| 0962  | [unique constraint](#rune-0962)  | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0963  | [upsert](#rune-0963)             | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0964  | [vacuum](#rune-0964)             | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |
| 0965  | [view {DB}](#rune-0965)          | A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.                                                                                     |
| 0966  | [WAL](#rune-0966)                | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0967  | [warehouse model](#rune-0967)    | A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.                                                                                                              |
| 0968  | [write skew {DB}](#rune-0968)    | A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.                                                                                          |

## Entries

### [0887] ACID {#rune-0887}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Pocket gloss:** The classic four-part law of transactional storage: atomicity, consistency, isolation, durability.

### [0888] append-only log {#rune-0888}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0889] archive table {#rune-0889}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0890] audit trail {#rune-0890}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0891] B-tree {#rune-0891}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0892] backup {#rune-0892}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Pocket gloss:** A restorable copy taken so loss is not final.

### [0893] base table {#rune-0893}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0894] bitmap index {#rune-0894}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0895] checkpoint {#rune-0895}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0896] column {DB} {#rune-0896}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0897] column store {#rune-0897}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0898] commit {DB} {#rune-0898}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** The moment a proposed change becomes durable history.

**Shadow:** writing too early, before the world is actually ready to carry the new truth.

**Pocket gloss:** Make a proposed change durable history.

### [0899] compaction {DB} {#rune-0899}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0900] conflict {#rune-0900}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0901] constraint {DB} {#rune-0901}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A bounding word; it removes degrees of freedom in order to improve correctness.

**Pocket gloss:** A rule the data is not allowed to violate.

### [0902] covering index {#rune-0902}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0903] cursor {DB} {#rune-0903}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0904] data lake {#rune-0904}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0905] data warehouse {#rune-0905}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0906] database {#rune-0906}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0907] deadlock {DB} {#rune-0907}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A binding curse; multiple actors wait on one another forever.

### [0908] denormalization {#rune-0908}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0909] dimension table {#rune-0909}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0910] durability {#rune-0910}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0911] durable log {#rune-0911}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0912] entity table {#rune-0912}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0913] event store {#rune-0913}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0914] fact table {#rune-0914}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0915] failover replica {#rune-0915}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0916] foreign key {#rune-0916}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

**Pocket gloss:** A declared reference from one table to another table's key.

### [0917] full table scan {#rune-0917}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0918] index {DB} {#rune-0918}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A retrieval word; it makes later access faster by storing an alternate path to data.

**Pocket gloss:** An access structure that speeds reads by paying extra write and space cost.

### [0919] index hint {#rune-0919}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0920] index seek {#rune-0920}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0921] isolation level {#rune-0921}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Pocket gloss:** The degree to which concurrent transactions are allowed to see one another's motion.

### [0922] join {DB} {#rune-0922}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A union word; separate streams, sets, or histories are brought into one result.

### [0923] keyspace {#rune-0923}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0924] LSM tree {#rune-0924}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0925] materialized view {#rune-0925}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

**Pocket gloss:** A stored query result traded for cheaper future reads.

### [0926] migration {#rune-0926}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Stored reality changing shape without being lost. Good migrations are staged, inspectable, and reversible long enough to discover what the data actually contains.

**Pocket gloss:** Change stored reality without losing it.

### [0927] MVCC {#rune-0927}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Pocket gloss:** Versioned rows that let readers and writers coexist more safely.

### [0928] nonclustered index {#rune-0928}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0929] OLAP {#rune-0929}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0930] OLTP {#rune-0930}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0931] partition {DB} {#rune-0931}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0932] partition key {#rune-0932}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0933] primary key {#rune-0933}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Pocket gloss:** The key that uniquely names one row inside its table.

### [0934] query plan {#rune-0934}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

**Pocket gloss:** The strategy the database chose to answer the query.

### [0935] read committed {#rune-0935}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0936] read replica {#rune-0936}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0937] record lock {#rune-0937}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0938] redo log {#rune-0938}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0939] relation {DB} {#rune-0939}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0940] repeatable read {#rune-0940}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0941] replication lag {#rune-0941}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0942] retention {#rune-0942}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0943] rollback {DB} {#rune-0943}

**Status:** canonical (major canon, pocket canon)

**Force:** Permission to retreat toward a previously trusted state. A system without rollback mistakes hope for safety.

**Pocket gloss:** Retreat toward a previously trusted state.

### [0944] row {DB} {#rune-0944}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0945] row store {#rune-0945}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0946] savepoint {#rune-0946}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0947] scan {DB} {#rune-0947}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0948] schema {DB} {#rune-0948}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A shape word for stored meaning.

**Pocket gloss:** The declared shape of database truth.

### [0949] secondary index {#rune-0949}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0950] seed data {#rune-0950}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0951] serializable {DB} {#rune-0951}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0952] sharding {#rune-0952}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0953] snapshot isolation {#rune-0953}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0954] sort key {#rune-0954}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0955] stored procedure {#rune-0955}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0956] table {DB} {#rune-0956}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0957] table scan {#rune-0957}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0958] tenant isolation {#rune-0958}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0959] transaction {#rune-0959}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** An all-or-nothing persistence word.

**Pocket gloss:** A unit of work meant to stand or fall together.

### [0960] TTL {#rune-0960}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0961] tuple {DB} {#rune-0961}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A durable-state word; it matters because the system must remember through change.

### [0962] unique constraint {#rune-0962}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0963] upsert {#rune-0963}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0964] vacuum {#rune-0964}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.

### [0965] view {DB} {#rune-0965}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A query-and-history word; it shapes how stored truth is accessed or preserved.

### [0966] WAL {#rune-0966}

**Status:** canonical (pocket canon)

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

**Pocket gloss:** Write-ahead log: history recorded before dirty pages reach their final home.

### [0967] warehouse model {#rune-0967}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A persistence-word; it binds state to time and query.

### [0968] write skew {DB} {#rune-0968}

**Status:** canonical

**Force:** A storage-law word; it governs how facts are kept, found, or rolled back.
